KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap --- ## PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each) ### Question 1 **[KTU University Exam – May 2024 Style]** **Q: Define a "Smart, Connected Assistive Device" (SCAD) and list its three defining characteristics.** **Model Answer (3 marks):** A Smart, Connected Assistive Device (SCAD) is a mechatronic medical device that integrates **sensing, computation, actuation, and wireless communication** to provide context-aware assistance to individuals with impairments, while operating within the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) framework. The three defining characteristics are: 1. **Context Awareness** — The ability to perceive the user's physiological state and surrounding environment using multimodal sensors. 2. **Connectivity** — Wireless data exchange with smartphones, cloud servers, or other devices for telemetry and remote monitoring. 3. **Adaptive Feedback** — Closed-loop control using AI/ML algorithms that modifies actuation in real time based on sensed data. *(3 marks — 1 for definition, 1 for naming the three features, 1 for brief explanation.)* --- ### Question 2 **[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023 Style]** **Q: Differentiate between Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) and LoRaWAN. Mention one assistive device application for each.** **Model Answer (3 marks):** | Parameter | BLE 5.0 | LoRaWAN | |---|---|---| | Range | ~100 m | Up to 10 km | | Data Rate | 2 Mbps | 0.3–50 kbps | | Power | Very low | Low (long sleep cycles) | | Topology | Star / Mesh | Star-of-stars | | Use case in SCAD | Smart hearing aid streaming | Rural fall detector with GPS | **BLE Application:** Myoelectric prosthetic hand streaming grip force data to a physiotherapist's tablet. **LoRaWAN Application:** A wearable fall detector worn by a cognitively impaired elderly person in a rural village, transmitting alerts over 5 km to a district hospital gateway. *(3 marks — 1 for table contrast, 1 for each example application.)* --- ## PART B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks Each, with Internal Choice) ### Question A (14 Marks) **[KTU University Exam – Model Paper 2024 Scheme]** **Q: Design a smart, connected fall detection system for elderly users. Your answer must cover:** **(a)** System architecture with all functional blocks and the choice of sensors. *(7 marks)* **(b)** Wireless communication strategy, cloud data flow, and power management plan. *(7 marks)* --- #### Part (a) — Model Solution (7 marks) **1. Problem Definition (1 mark):** Falls are the leading cause of injury-related death in adults over 65. A SCAD must detect falls **within 1 second**, send an alert to caregivers, and operate **> 7 days** on a single charge. **2. Sensor Selection (2 marks):** A **6-axis IMU (MPU-6050)** at 100 Hz is used as the primary sensor, providing 3-axis accelerometer ($\pm 16$ g range) and 3-axis gyroscope ($\pm 2000$ °/s). - **Accelerometer** detects the sudden impact (jerk $>$ 2g) and the post-fall orientation (tilt $>$ 60° from vertical). - **Gyroscope** detects the angular velocity spike during the descent phase. - **Barometric pressure sensor (BMP280)** confirms fall via altitude change $>$ 0.5 m. - **Heart rate sensor (MAX30102)** monitors post-fall vitals to differentiate a real fall from a sudden drop of the device. **3. Architecture (4 marks):** ``` [IMU + Baro + HR Sensor] → [Signal Conditioning] → [STM32L4 MCU] → [Fall Detection Algorithm (Threshold + ML)] → [Alert Engine] ├── [BLE Module] → [Smartphone App] → [Caregiver SMS] └── [LoRa Backup] → [Cloud Gateway] ``` The STM32L4 MCU runs a two-stage algorithm: - **Stage 1 (Threshold):** Acceleration magnitude $> 2g$ and tilt $> 60°$ for $> 5$ s. - **Stage 2 (ML verification):** 1D-CNN with 50 ms sliding window confirms fall vs. ADL (Activities of Daily Living) with 97% accuracy (research benchmark). --- #### Part (b) — Model Solution (7 marks) **1. Wireless Strategy (2 marks):** - **Primary link — BLE 5.0:** Smartphones are ubiquitous; the device pairs with the user's phone for 24/7 connectivity. Uses GATT profile with custom "Fall Alert" characteristic. - **Secondary link — LoRaWAN (SF9, BW125):** Activated only when the smartphone is out of range (e.g., user leaves phone in another room). Ensures "always-on" alerting. - **Tertiary link — GPS (NEO-6M):** Provides location coordinates of the fall, transmitted along with the alert packet. **2. Cloud Data Flow (3 marks):** - Smartphone publishes an MQTT message to AWS IoT Core over TLS 1.3. - Topic structure: `elderly//fall/event`. - Message body (JSON, FHIR-compliant): ```json { "timestamp": "2024-09-12T08:14:22Z", "user_id": "EL-1029", "event_type": "FALL_CONFIRMED", "severity": "HIGH", "location": {"lat": 10.0261, "lon": 76.3125}, "vitals": {"hr": 112, "spo2": 94} } ``` - AWS Lambda triggers **SNS** to send SMS to the caregiver and **DynamoDB** stores the event for longitudinal analysis. A clinician dashboard (React + Chart.js) plots fall frequency vs. medication changes. **3. Power Management (2 marks):** - **Battery:** 3.7 V, 400 mAh Li-Po (1.48 Wh). - **Current profile (duty-cycled):** - Active sensing: 8 mA for 10% duty cycle - BLE transmit: 12 mA for 2% duty cycle - Deep sleep: 0.05 mA for 88% duty cycle - **Average current:** $$ I_{avg} = (8 \times 0.10) + (12 \times 0.02) + (0.05 \times 0.88) $$ $$ I_{avg} = 0.80 + 0.24 + 0.044 $$ $$ I_{avg} = 1.084 \text{ mA} $$ - **Runtime:** $$ T = \frac{400}{1.084} = 369 \text{ hours} \approx 15.4 \text{ days} $$ - This **exceeds the 7-day target** with a safety margin. A small solar cell (0.5 W) on the wristband can extend runtime indefinitely via energy harvesting. --- ### Question B (14 Marks) — *Alternative Choice* **[KTU University Exam – Sessional Model Paper 2024]** **Q: With a neat block diagram, explain the architecture of a myoelectric (EMG-controlled) smart prosthetic hand. Discuss:** **(a)** Signal acquisition, feature extraction, and gesture classification pipeline. *(7 marks)* **(b)** Real-time actuation, closed-loop feedback, and integration with a smartphone app. *(7 marks)* --- #### Part (a) — Model Solution (7 marks) **1. Acquisition (2 marks):** - Two pairs of surface EMG (sEMG) electrodes are placed on the **flexor digitorum superficialis** and **extensor digitorum** muscles of the residual limb. - Raw EMG (amplitude 50 µV – 5 mV, bandwidth 20–500 Hz) is amplified with gain = 1000 and bandpass filtered (4th-order Butterworth, 20–450 Hz). - Sampled at $f_s = 1$ kHz with 12-bit ADC, satisfying the Nyquist criterion with 20% margin. **2. Feature Extraction (2 marks):** - 250 ms sliding window with 128-sample increment (75% overlap). - Features computed per channel: **MAV, RMS, Waveform Length (WL), Zero Crossings (ZC), Slope Sign Changes (SSC)**. - Combined into a 10-dimensional feature vector (5 features × 2 channels). **3. Classification (3 marks):** - A lightweight **1D-CNN** (3 conv layers, 2 dense layers) classifies 5 gestures: open, close, pinch, key grip, and rest. - Trained on the **Ninapro DB2** public dataset, achieving 96.4% accuracy. - Inference time on STM32H7: **6.2 ms**, leaving ample time within the 50 ms control loop. --- #### Part (b) — Model Solution (7 marks) **1. Actuation (2 marks):** - Five **micro servo motors (DS3235)** drive individual fingers via tendon cables. - Each servo receives a **PWM command (50 Hz, 1–2 ms pulse width)** mapped from the classifier output (0 = open, 1 = closed). - Position feedback from a **rotary encoder** on each motor closes the position loop. **2. Closed-Loop Control (2 marks):** - **PID controller** with $K_p = 1.2$, $K_i = 0.05$, $K_d = 0.1$ tuned via Ziegler-Nichols. - **Adaptive gain scheduling** modifies gains based on object weight (estimated from motor current draw). - **Safety layer:** If motor current exceeds 800 mA for > 2 s, the controller enters "soft stop" to protect the user and motor. **3. Smartphone Integration (3 marks):** - The hand broadcasts a **BLE GATT server** with three services: grip force, battery level, and gesture log. - A **Flutter app** (Android/iOS) displays real-time force, allows gesture calibration, and uploads session data to a **Firebase** backend. - Clinicians access a **dashboard** to monitor usage patterns — e.g., average grips per day — informing prosthetic therapy. --- > [!WARNING] > **KTU Examiner's Valuation Pitfall — Common Marks Lost:** > 1. **Confusing BLE with "Bluetooth Classic"** — They are different protocols. BLE is for low-power sensor data; Classic is for audio streaming. > 2. **Forgetting to mention the Nyquist criterion** when justifying sampling rates. Examiners specifically check for this. > 3. **Drawing block diagrams without arrow labels** — Every block must have signal/data flow arrows with clear labels (e.g., "EMG (µV)" not just "→"). > 4. **Ignoring security/privacy (HIPAA/GDPR)** in cloud-based designs. Even a single line about AES-256 / TLS earns partial marks. > 5. **Computing battery runtime without showing the duty cycle math** — Always write the three-term sum and then divide. > 6. **Mixing up LoRaWAN range with Wi-Fi range** — LoRa is km-scale; Wi-Fi is room-scale. Examiners are strict on units. --- ## Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember - A **SCAD** is defined by **three A's** — Acquisition (sensors), Adaptation (intelligence), Access (connectivity). - The **Nyquist criterion** $f_s \geq 2 f_{max}$ governs all biosignal ADC designs (ECG 500 Hz, EMG 1 kHz, EEG 256 Hz). - The **link budget equation** $P_{rx} = P_{tx} + G_t + G_r - L_{path}$ and **FSPL formula** are essential for wireless SCAD design. - **Battery runtime** is computed as $T = C / I_{avg}$ where $I_{avg}$ is the **duty-cycle-weighted sum** of all current states. - **BLE** is preferred for wearables, **LoRaWAN** for rural/long-range, **Wi-Fi** for home hubs, **NB-IoT/5G** for cellular connectivity. - **IoMT architecture** has 5 functional layers (Perception, Edge, Network, Cloud, Application) + 1 cross-cutting Security layer. - **MQTT** is the de-facto messaging protocol for SCAD cloud telemetry; **FHIR** is the healthcare data standard. - **RTOS** (FreeRTOS, Zephyr) enables deterministic real-time response in safety-critical assistive devices. - **Closed-loop control** (PID) is the backbone of prosthetic and wheelchair actuation; **AI/ML** enhances gesture recognition and fall detection. - **Power management** strategies: duty cycling, sleep modes, and energy harvesting (solar, thermoelectric, kinetic). - **Security/Privacy:** End-to-end encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3), compliance with **HIPAA** (US), **GDPR** (EU), and **DISHA** (India). - **Standards:** ISO 9999 (assistive products classification), IEC 60601-1 (medical electrical equipment safety), IEEE 11073 (personal health device communication). - **Key trend (2024+):** Edge-AI inference on-device using TinyML, enabling privacy-preserving, low-latency smart assistive devices without cloud dependency. > [!TIP] > **Final Exam Mantra:** Always structure long answers as — **Block Diagram → Sensor List → Algorithm → Communication → Power → Security → Validation Metric.** This six-step template covers every marking layer in KTU's analytical-style questions.
